skill.name: database-migrations-sql-migrations
skill.description: Create safe SQL database migration plans with zero-downtime patterns, validation checks, rollback procedures, and framework examples.
skill.summary: Plans production-ready SQL migrations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are Markdown SQL, Python, and Bash examples misclassified as executable backticks, weak cryptography, sensitive files, or reconnaissance. No evidence found of malware, credential exfiltration, network activity, or prompt-injection override text. Publish with warnings because copied migration examples include shell and dynamic SQL patterns that require validation before production use.

medium_findings.title: Copy-Paste Shell Rollback Script Requires Hardening
medium_findings.description: The rollback example runs psql and pg_dump with shell variables and interpolates MIGRATION_VERSION into SQL. The skill does not execute this code, but users copying it should quote arguments and validate version values before production use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash commands are present in a Markdown example and are not executed automatically. The command and SQL interpolation risks are real if a user copies the example with untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic SQL Identifier Interpolation in Examples
medium_findings.description: Several Python examples compose SQL with table or cursor identifiers. This is acceptable for trusted migration specifications, but untrusted identifiers need allowlists or database-specific identifier quoting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The f-string SQL construction is clear, but the examples appear intended for trusted internal migration metadata. Risk depends on whether user-controlled identifiers reach these snippets.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts point to fenced SQL or Python examples, not Ruby backtick execution. No evidence found that these examples run during skill loading or activation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown fences, SQL examples, Python examples, and resource references. They are documentation content rather than executable Ruby or shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not identify hashing or encryption logic. The referenced lines contain a SQL query for migration version lookup and the skill description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic API or algorithm is present at the referenced lines. The finding appears caused by keyword matching in database migration text.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Database Examples
low_findings.description: The sensitive SQLite and reconnaissance alerts point to database connection variables, SQL reads, row counts, bounds checks, and loop variables. No evidence found of host reconnaissance or local database file collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced context is migration logic that reads application database state. It does not enumerate the host, secrets, files, or networks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copy-Paste Shell Rollback Script Requires Hardening
dangerous_patterns.description: The rollback example runs psql and pg_dump with shell variables and interpolates MIGRATION_VERSION into SQL. The skill does not execute this code, but users copying it should quote arguments and validate version values before production use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash commands are present in a Markdown example and are not executed automatically. The command and SQL interpolation risks are real if a user copies the example with untrusted input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic SQL Identifier Interpolation in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several Python examples compose SQL with table or cursor identifiers. This is acceptable for trusted migration specifications, but untrusted identifiers need allowlists or database-specific identifier quoting.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The f-string SQL construction is clear, but the examples appear intended for trusted internal migration metadata. Risk depends on whether user-controlled identifiers reach these snippets.

content.value_statement: Production database changes can cause downtime, data loss, or failed releases. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design migration plans with validation, rollback, and zero-downtime patterns.
actual_capabilities: Designs expand-contract and blue-green migration strategies for SQL databases.
Drafts migration scripts for Flyway, Alembic, and direct SQL workflows.
Adds pre-migration and post-migration validation checks for data integrity.
Plans rollback procedures with snapshots, transactions, and manual recovery steps.
Suggests batch processing, indexing, and parallel migration approaches for large tables.
limitations: Does not connect to databases or verify live schema state by itself.
Generated scripts require review against the target engine and version.
Rollback plans may need adaptation for irreversible data transformations.
Examples are guidance and should not be copied into production without hardening.

use_cases.title: Release a Backward-Compatible Schema Change
use_cases.description: Plan an expand-contract rollout that keeps old and new application versions working during deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Production Migration Runbook
use_cases.description: Create validation, backup, rollback, monitoring, and ownership steps for a scheduled database change.

use_cases.title: Improve Migration Safety in CI/CD
use_cases.description: Convert a risky schema change into reviewed migration steps with automated checks and deployment gates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Migration Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small schema change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a safe SQL migration for adding [column or table] to [database engine]. Include validation and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Zero-Downtime Rollout
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate migration with active application traffic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an expand-contract migration for [schema change]. Assume [traffic level], [table size], and [deployment process].

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Migration Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this migration plan for downtime, locking, data integrity, rollback, and monitoring risks: [paste plan].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Production Runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a production runbook for [migration]. Include prerequisites, owner checklist, validation queries, rollback decision points, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Add email verification status to a large users table without downtime.
output: A phased expand-contract plan that adds the new column safely.
A batch backfill approach with progress checks and throttling.
Validation and rollback notes for release approval.

output_examples.input: Review a migration that renames a heavily used orders column.
output: Risk notes covering application compatibility and locking behavior.
A safer dual-write or compatibility-column strategy.
A release sequence for application and database changes.

output_examples.input: Create a production runbook for moving data to a new table.
output: Rollback triggers, communication steps, and monitoring points.

best_practices: Test migrations on production-sized data before scheduling a release.
Separate additive schema changes, data backfills, and cleanup changes into phases.
Define rollback decision points before executing irreversible operations.
anti_patterns: Running destructive schema changes in the same release as application code changes.
Copying generated shell scripts without quoting arguments and validating inputs.
Backfilling large tables in one transaction without batching or monitoring.

faq.question: Does this skill run database migrations?
faq.answer: No. It produces plans, scripts, checklists, and review guidance for a human or automation system to execute.

faq.question: Which databases does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on SQL migrations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server, with related framework examples.

faq.question: Can it guarantee zero downtime?
faq.answer: No. It can design safer strategies, but downtime risk depends on schema size, locks, engine behavior, and deployment process.

faq.question: Can it write rollback scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. Review generated rollback scripts carefully because some data transformations cannot be fully reversed.

faq.question: Should I use this for production changes?
faq.answer: Yes, as planning and review support. A qualified engineer should test and approve every production migration.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains shell and dynamic SQL examples. They are not executed automatically, but copied examples need hardening.
